prose:
Method provenance block:
Property: Frontier orbital energies and HOMO–LUMO gap of formaldehyde (gap reported)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), density-fitted, as implemented in PySCF 2.x via chemkit
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz); single-point calculation — no further geometry relaxation (DFT//user-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model applied)
Charge and multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF backend (SCF converged in 8 cycles)
Convergence + caveats: SCF converged; no warnings reported by engine; density fitting (RI/JK) used by default in this tier. Live PySCF output was streamed to the automatically generated frontier-orbitals .out log file in the working directory (path printed at run start and stored alongside the JSON result).

Result (gas phase):
HOMO energy = −7.6523067 eV
LUMO energy = −1.7826355 eV
Computed HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV

All values come directly from the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill run described above. No engine warnings were reported. Integrity check status: ok (trustworthy = true).
